meaning: n.[天](日蚀、月蚀或太阳黑子的)半影
examples: This includes the continuous survey of umbra and penumbra of the sunspot.
In the outer part of that shadow, the penumbra, part of the Sun, is blocked.
Moon enters penumbra: instant of first external tangency of moon with the penumbra.
They have sought to find a place for Australia in the penumbra of Asia.
As Sigismund Blondy saw him, Zwelish walked in a fiery aura of loneliness, but Blondy had got inside the penumbra.
